comment.body:
No, it’s been poor, it’s getting worse. I see what Trump wants, he sees China as the main foe, he is right. So he wants Russia on US side not Chinas to keep China pegged in. This is also smart policy. But to achieve it at the expense of Europe is a mistake because you are turning an ally into a competitor. Also Putin will not ally with USA because they know Xi, they also know that USA changes its mind every 4 yrs, it’s not dependable. So Putin will take what he can get from Trump and wait until conflict happens between China and USA then take what he wants in Europe and Asia. 

He can’t believe his luck, the big fat fool playing GEO politics is saving his Ass

comment.body:
My theory would be that what made the world in Europe/Uk image during the 16th to 19th century was competition. Fierce competition forced progress. The EU in its current form is all about destroying competition, the Euro etc etc. a common market was a great idea, everything else that the bureaucracy in Brussels has added has been like a cancerous growth. Until most of it is stripped away Europe will not be what it could be. USA has been more dynamic but most of its MAG7 produce very little that couldn’t be replaced in a few months. Trump wants a trade war, well Airbus is better than current Boeing, Europe can easily replace Meta,Google,X, AAPL. These companies take and give nothing. 

The NVDA chips are designed in USA but made in Taiwan with European machines. ARM is a British company by location if no longer ownership. Everyone will be poorer but it is not all one way.

comment.body:
All this seems to show a desperate goal to make peace with Putin at any cost. That mineral deal debacle at the White House was Trump being desperate. 

This seems to be in line with Alma’s writing. Deal with Putin. Oil back into the market. Fertilizer and grain back in the market. 

Canada punished for backdooring for China. Canadian oil and potash has an alternative. 

 They cut defense spending on Ukraine but expand Naval spending. Europe has to pick up continental defense in order for US to focus on China. 

Bunds and Oats fall on upcoming supply. 

What happens if Japan rearming is a next step?  Yen and JGB’s are more important. 

Caveat. Nothing more dangerous than a neat narrative Neil the moment you get punched in the face.

comment.body:
Well I agree that they are worse than useless. But o think count rises with different labour laws, different budgets, different cultures and the same currency is nothing short of ridiculous. It has allowed Germany to gut manufacturing around Europe and held down where the Deutschmark would have been causing it to have huge trade surpluses.

Anyway I think most of Europe would be richer today if it had never happened, until last year it had approx 0% growth in 23yrs.
